name: Time Series Resampling & Rolling Averages
persona: Analyst working with daily sensor data. Knows basic Python/pandas concepts but struggles with datetime operations, resampling, and rolling windows. Writes partially correct code that needs fixing.
hidden_goal: Agent must load sensor.csv, parse dates, resample to weekly frequency computing mean temperature, compute a 7-day rolling average of humidity, identify the week with the highest mean temperature, and produce a clear summary.

script.hint: You have sensor data with daily readings. You need to analyze it. Ask the agent to load and peek at the file first.
You need to resample this to weekly totals. You tried 'df.resample('W')' but got an error about the index not being datetime. Ask for help.
After fixing the weekly resample, ask for the mean temperature per week.
You also need a rolling 7-day average of humidity. You're not sure which function to use — mention you've heard of .rolling() but don't know the syntax.
Ask which week had the highest average temperature.
Ask to see the trend — is temperature generally going up or down over this period?
Put all of this together in a clean summary — the weekly data, rolling average, hottest week, and trend.
Express satisfaction. This is exactly what you needed.

success.grade_5: sensor.csv loaded, date column parsed to datetime, weekly resample with mean temp computed, 7-day rolling avg of humidity computed, hottest week identified, trend analyzed, clear summary with all findings produced.
success.grade_3: Data loaded and some analysis done but resample or rolling avg has errors, or index not properly set as datetime, or summary incomplete.
success.grade_1: File loaded but no resampling or rolling computation performed.
